title: Memory leak regression in v2.14.0 (~30-50 MiB/day, OOM after ~10 days)
author: AKhozya
body:
## Description

Slow memory leak introduced in **v2.14.0**, causing OOM kill after ~10 days of uptime. Not present in v2.12.0–v2.13.2 which were stable for weeks.

## Environment

- **Image**: `frooodle/s-pdf:2.14.2-fat` (also reproduced on v2.14.0-fat)
- **Platform**: Kubernetes (K3s), linux/amd64
- **JVM args**: `-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=45`
- **Memory limit**: 1280 MiB (cgroup)

## Evidence (30-day Prometheus data)

| Date range | Version | Memory (MiB) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 10–24 | v2.12.0 → v2.13.2 | 752–912 | Stable baseline ~860 MiB |
| Jun 25–30 | v2.13.2 | 827–871 | Stable |
| **Jul 1** | **v2.14.0 deployed** | 854 → 921 → 954 | **Uptick begins** |
| Jul 2–8 | v2.14.0 → v2.14.2 | 849 → 903 | Slow climb ~30-50 MiB/day |
| Jul 9–10 | v2.14.2 | 953 → 972 → 1217 | **OOM killed** (exit code 255) |

The pod ran for 2 days after restart and was already climbing again when we bumped the limit.

## Relation to #6211

This is a **different leak** from the v2.10.0 regression (#6211). That was an immediate spike on startup; this is a slow, steady climb starting from a normal baseline. v2.11.0 through v2.13.2 did not exhibit this behavior.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy `frooodle/s-pdf:2.14.2-fat` with `-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=45`
2. Monitor RSS/working-set over several days
3. Observe ~30-50 MiB/day growth (no user activity required — idle leak)

## Expected Behavior

Memory should stabilize around ~860 MiB as it did on v2.12.0–v2.13.2.

title: [Bug]: Blank window on launch (EGL_BAD_PARAMETER) — AppImage on Fedora KDE Wayland
author: corrigo21
body:
### Installation Method

Local Installation

### The Problem

## Bug: Blank window on launch (EGL_BAD_PARAMETER) — AppImage on Fedora KDE Wayland

**Affected versions:** 2.13.2 and 2.14.0 (both AppImage builds)
**OS:** Fedora 44 KDE Plasma, Wayland session
**Mesa:** 26.1.3-1.fc44
**Install method:** Official Linux AppImage (`Stirling-PDF-linux-x86_64.appimage`)

### Problem
On launch, the Tauri window either:
- crashes immediately with `WebKitWebProcess` receiving SIGABRT (coredump confirms `abort()` from libc), or
- opens but stays completely blank, until manually closed.

In every case, the console shows:
```bash
Could not create default EGL display: EGL_BAD_PARAMETER. Aborting...
```
This happens on **both v2.13.2 and v2.14.0** AppImages, so it's not a regression in a specific release.

Meanwhile the bundled Java backend (Spring Boot) starts up correctly every time and serves fine on its local port — this is purely a WebKitGTK/Wayland rendering issue in the Tauri shell, unrelated to the backend.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Download `Stirling-PDF-linux-x86_64.appimage` (v2.13.2 or v2.14.0)
2. `chmod +x` and run it from a terminal on Fedora 44 KDE (Wayland)
3. Observe blank window / WebKitWebProcess crash

### What did NOT fix it
```bash
WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 ./Stirling-PDF-linux-x86_64.appimage
GDK_BACKEND=x11 WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 ./Stirling-PDF-linux-x86_64.appimage
```
Both still produced blank windows / the same EGL_BAD_PARAMETER message.

### What DID fix it
Forcing the system's 64-bit `libwayland-client` via `LD_PRELOAD`, in addition to the WebKit env vars:
```bash
WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 LD_PRELOAD=/lib64/libwayland-client.so.0 ./Stirling-PDF-linux-x86_64.appimage
```
This confirms the AppImage's bundled `libwayland-client` (inside the squashfs) is conflicting with the system's on Fedora, causing the EGL init failure.

**Note on Fedora multilib:** `LD_PRELOAD=/lib/libwayland-client.so.0` (the 32-bit path on Fedora, since `/lib` → 32-bit and `/lib64` → 64-bit) fails silently with `wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32` and falls back to the exact same crash. Only the explicit `/lib64/...` path works. This distinction should probably be documented, or the AppImage launcher could auto-detect and try both paths (similar to what other Tauri-based apps have implemented, e.g. https://github.com/refactoringhq/tolaria/commit/4f7f5a3).

### Suggested fix
Consider having the AppImage launcher:
1. Set `WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1` and `WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1` by default on Linux AppImage builds (unless already set by the user), and
2. Auto-detect and preload the system's Wayland client library (checking `/lib64`, `/usr/lib64`, `/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu`, matching architecture) when running under a Wayland session.

### Logs
[full terminal logs available on request / attached]

2.14.0 

### Last Working Version of Stirling-PDF

_No response_

### Page Where the Problem Occurred

_No response_

title: Tessdata prefix is hardcoded in the app (Linux)
author: LFd3v
body:
Hey, there.

First of all, thank you for creating and maintaining the (free and OSS) app! I forgot to thank you in the previous issue I opened, sorry.

This is more like a heads up, not exactly a bug: it seems that the app looks for Tesseract .trainedata files only in the Ubuntu (Debian too, probably?) default location: `/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata`, both the AppImage and the AUR file, that is based on the .deb package, cannot file the files in the default installation on ArchLinux, that is simply `/usr/share/tessdata`.

The good news is that it honors `TESSDATA_PREFIX` environment variable, so launching the app while setting `TESSDATA_PREFIX=/usr/share/tessdata` actually works.

So I thought I would just add an issue here to help other users, and maybe this can be added to the documentation, although I did not find a good place where it could be added.

Thanks again. Regards

title: [Bug]: JPDFium native library fails to load in Debian 13 LXC due to executable stack requirement
author: MickLesk
body:
### Installation Method

Local Installation

### The Problem

Merge/Split fails in an unprivileged Debian 13 LXC container after upgrading Stirling-PDF past v2.11.0.

This is related to the new JPDFium/native PDFium backend introduced in v2.12.0+. The bundled native library fails to load inside LXC because one of the extracted native libraries requires an executable stack.

Reference downstream issue in community-scripts:

https://github.com/community-scripts/ProxmoxVE/issues/15513

The affected install is deployed as a bare .jar via systemd, not Docker.

### Version of Stirling-PDF

v2.14.0

### Last Working Version of Stirling-PDF

v.2.10.x

### Page Where the Problem Occurred

Merge PDFs / Split PDFs

### Docker Configuration

```txt
Not Docker.

Local installation inside Debian 13 LXC on Proxmox VE 9.2.3.
```

### Relevant Log Output

```txt
Caused by: java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: /tmp/jpdfium-/libjpdfium.so: libicudata.so.74: cannot enable executable stack as shared object requires: Invalid argument at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.NativeLibraries.load(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.NativeLibraries$NativeLibraryImpl.open(NativeLibraries.java:321) at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.NativeLibraries.loadLibrary(NativeLibraries.java:187) at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.NativeLibraries.loadLibrary(NativeLibraries.java:129) at java.base/java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary(ClassLoader.java:2245) at java.base/java.lang.Runtime.load0(Runtime.java:767) at java.base/java.lang.System.load(System.java:1647) at stirling.software.jpdfium.panama.NativeLoader.tryLoadFromClasspath(NativeLoader.java:103) at stirling.software.jpdfium.panama.NativeLoader.ensureLoaded(NativeLoader.java:30) Caused by: stirling.software.jpdfium.exception.NativeLoadException: Failed to load native library at stirling.software.jpdfium.panama.NativeLoader.ensureLoaded(NativeLoader.java:44) at stirling.software.jpdfium.panama.JpdfiumLib.<init>(JpdfiumLib.java:45) at stirling.software.jpdfium.PdfDocument.open(PdfDocument.java:31) at stirling.software.SPDF.controller.api.MergeController.mergePdfs(MergeController.java:307)


readelf -lW confirms that the affected native library has an executable stack requirement:

GNU_STACK 0x000000 0x0000000000000000 0x0000000000000000 0x000000 0x000000 RWE 0x10
```

### Additional Information

This is reproducible on a fresh Debian 13 LXC installation.

Downstream we tried patching system ICU libraries during installation:
```bash
msg_info "Patching Native Libraries for LXC Compatibility"
ensure_dependencies patchelf
find /usr/lib -name "libicudata.so.*" -exec patchelf --clear-execstack {} \; || true
msg_ok "Patched Native Libraries"
```

however, fresh installs still fail because the problematic native library is bundled/extracted by JPDFium at runtime, so patching system libraries is not sufficient.

The issue does not appear to be caused by missing dependencies. ldd resolves the native library dependencies cleanly.

Also tested:
`GLIBC_TUNABLES=glibc.cpu.hwcaps=-SHSTK`

This did not resolve the issue.
This looks like an upstream packaging/build problem in the bundled native JPDFium/PDFium library. Ideally, the native library should be shipped without requiring an executable stack, or JPDFium should provide a container-compatible build.



### Browsers Affected

Other

### No Duplicate of the Issue

title: [Bug]: Arrow and polyline annotation tools inaccessible - no UI buttons despite backend support
author: liamchesters
body:
### Installation Method

Docker fat

### The Problem

The embedPDF functionality includes support for arrow and polyline annotations, however there are no corresponding buttons in the UI to access these tools, making them completely inaccessible to users.

Arrow and polyline annotation tools should be accessible via the UI and these tools are mentioned in the Stirling PDF documentation.

### Version of Stirling-PDF

2.13.2

### Last Working Version of Stirling-PDF

_No response_

### Page Where the Problem Occurred

http://localhost:8080/annotate

### Docker Configuration

```txt

```

### Relevant Log Output

```txt

```

### Additional Information

I have implemented a fix for this, adding the missing buttons and icons for both tools and am happy to submit these as a PR.

### Browsers Affected

Firefox, Microsoft Edge

### No Duplicate of the Issue

- [x] I have verified that there are no existing issues raised related to my problem.

title: [Bug]: Bookmarks and Attachments not loading correctly
author: MattSaito
body: ### Installation Method _No response_ ### The Problem When working with PDFs that contain bookmarks or attachments, these items are sometimes not loaded correctly after certain actions. This seems to happen after operations that save and reload the PDF, such as adding new attachments. It can also happen when switching between PDFs using the left file navigation sidebar. **Example:** Considering two PDFs that both contain bookmarks and attachments: PDF 1 : PDF 2 : Navigating to another pdf using the files left bar(1) , leads to the bookmarks and attachments not being loaded correctly (2): ### Version of Stirling-PDF 2.13.2 ### Last Working Version of Stirling-PDF _No response_ ### Page Where the Problem Occurred _No response_ ### Docker Configuration ```txt ``` ### Relevant Log Output ```txt ``` ### Additional Information Navigation through the **Active files** tab does not seem to have the same problem, as bookmarks and attachments are loaded correctly when switching files from there. ### Browsers Affected _No response_ ### No Duplicate of the Issue - [x] I have verified that there are no existing issues raised related to my problem.
